verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 encodes HSP60, canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin essential for protein folding. However, HSP60 is well-documented to translocate to the plasma membrane outer leaflet in multiple non-baseline contexts: (1) tumor cells constitutively display HSP60 on the cell surface (cancer ecto-chaperone, documented by flow cytometry and surface biotinylation on many tumor lines); (2) immunogenic / apoptotic cell death redistributes HSP60 to the outer leaflet; (3) LPS-activated immune cells surface-expose HSP60. Multiple CAR-T and antibody programs target surface HSP60 on tumor cells. The protein has no GPI anchor or TM domain of its own, so surface display is entirely cell-state-induced (stress, transformation, apoptosis). This firmly maps to contextual / cell_state_induced. The dominant baseline compartment is mitochondrial matrix, but the cancer-cell and cell-death ecto-pool is well-established.
